sections.paragraph:
fut. יִכְרֹת-

(1) to cut, to cut off, as part of a garment [“comp. Sanscr. Krit.”] 1Sa 24:5, 12 1Sa 24:12the branch of a tree, Num 13:23, 24 Num 13:24 præputium, Exo 4:25 the head, 1Sa 17:51 5:4 to cut down trees, Deu 19:5 Isa 14:8 44:14 Jerem. 10:3 22:7 46:23 (whence כֹּרְתֵי הָעֵצִים woodcutters, 2Ch 2:9); images of false gods, Exo 34:13 Jud. 6:25 , 26 Jdg 6:26, 30 Jdg 6:30 כָּרוּת Levit. 22:24 more fully כְּרוּת שָׁפְכָה Deu 23:2, castrated.

(2) to kill, to destroy persons, Deu 20:20 Jer 11:19. Niph. and Hiph.

(3) specially כָּרַת בְּרִית; Gr. ὅρκια τέμνειν, τέμνειν σπονδὰς, to make a covenant, so used from slaying and dividing the victims, as was customary in making a covenant (see Gen 15:18 Jer 34:8, 18 Jer 34:18comp. Bochart, Hieroz. t. i. lib. 2, cap. 35; Danzii Interpres, p. 255; also Gr. σπονδὴ, libation, league, whence is spondere). Commonly construed followed by עִם and (אִתּ) אֵת with any one, Gen 15:18 Exo 24:8 Deu 4:23 5:3 and so frequently; but followed by לְ-(a) where the more powerful party prescribes the terms of the covenant to the other. 2Ki 11:4 poet. Job 31:1, בְּרִית כָּרַתִּי לְעֵינַי “I made a covenant with my eyes,” i.e. prescribed these terms to them (compare 2Sa 5:3 1Ch 11:3); hence used of Jehovah establishing a covenant with men, 2Ch 21:7 Isa 55:3 61:8 Jer 32:40.-(b) where the victor concedes the benefit of peace, and a league to the vanquished. Jos 9:6, עַתָּה כִּרְתוּ לָנוּ בְרִית “now grant a league to us;” verse Jos 9:7, sq.; 1Sa 11:1, 1Sa 11:2 Exo 23:32 34:12 , 15 Exo 34:15 Deu 7:2.-(c) where any thing is vowed to God. Ezr 10:3, לֵאלֹהֵינוּ לְהֹוצִיא כָל־נָשִׁים וְעַתָּה נִכְרָת־בְּרִית “now then let us vow to our God to put away all the wives;” hence כָּרַת דָּבָר to vow, to promise any thing, Hag 2:5. In all these phrases בְּרִית may also be omitted. 1Sa 11:2 20:16 22:8 2Ch 7:18 Isa 57:8, וַתִּכְרָת־לָךְ מֵהֶם “and thou hast joined with thee (some) of them in covenant;” Vulg. fœdus pepigisti cum eis. For בְּרִית there once occurs the syn. אֲמָנָה Neh 10:1.

Niph.-

(1) to be cut down, as a tree, Job 14:7 Isa 55:13. Figuratively to be cut off from one’s country, i.e. to be driven into exile, to be expelled, Zec 14:2.

(2) to be extirpated, destroyed, used of persons, Gen 9:11 Psa 37:9 Pro 2:22 10:31 , and so often. Thus, in the customary expression of the Mosaic law, נִכְרְתָה הַנֶּפֶשׁ הַהִיא מֵעַמֶּיהָ “that soul (that person) shall be cut off from his people,” Gen 17:14 Levit. 7:20 , 21 עַמָּם, מִקֶּרֶב עַמֹּו Levit. 17:4 , Lev 17:9 18:29 20:18 Num 15:30 מִיִּשְׂרָאֵל Exo 12:15 Num 19:13 מִתֹּוךְ הַקָּהָל Num 19:20 מֵעֲדַת יִשְׂרָאֵל Exo 12:19 and simpl. נִכְרְתָה הַנֶּפֶשׁ הַהִיא Levit. 17:14 20:17 . By this phrase is meant the punishment of death in general, without any definition of the manner (never the punishment of exile, as is supposed by J. D. Michaëlis, on the Mosaic Law, v. § 237), Exo 31:14 compare Exo 35:2 and Num 15:32. [In some of the passages it appears only to signify severed from the congregation of the Lord.] Hence-

(3) to perish, to fail, i.q. אָבַד. 1Ki 2:4, לֹא־יִכָּרֵת לְךָ אִישׁ מֵעַל כִּסֵּא יִשְׂרָאֵל “there shall not fail thee (perish unto thee) a man from off the throne of Israel;” 1Ki 8:25 9:5 Isa 48:19 Jer 33:17, 18 Jer 33:18 35:19 used of a country (people) perishing through famine, Gen 41:36 of a name, Rth 4:10 of hope, Pro 23:18 24:14 of fidelity, Jer 7:28 Jos 9:23, לֹא־יִכָּרֵת מִכֶּם עֶבֶד “there shall not fail (i.e. cease) from you a slave;” i.e. ye shall be slaves for ever. 2Sa 3:29.

(4) to be cut off, i.e. divided; used of the waters of Jordan, Jos 3:13 4:7 compare כְּרִיתוּת divorce.

(5) to be consumed as food, Num 11:33.

Pual כֹּרַת and כָּרַּת to be cut off, or down, Jud. 6:28 . Eze 16:4.

Hiphil-

(1) i.q. Kal No. 2, to kill, destroy men, Lev 17:10 nations, Jos 23:4 Zep 3:6 Eze 25:7 instruments of idolatry, Lev 26:30.

(2) to separate, to remove, to withdraw. 1Sa 20:15, וְלֹא־תַכְרִית אֶת־חַסְדְּךָ מֵעִם בֵּיתִי “that thou withdraw not thy mercy from my house.”

Hophal הָכְרַת to be cut off from something, followed by מִן Joe 1:9.

The derived nouns immediately follow, except כְּרִית, כְּרִיתֻת.
